name: OPEC+
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **UAE OUT OF CARTEL EFFECTIVE MAY 1; OPEC+ PROCEEDED WITH SYMBOLIC 188 KBPD ADJUSTMENT MAY 3 WITHOUT UAE.** Goldman Sachs CUT Q2 2026 Brent forecast to $90/bbl (from $99) and WTI to $87 (from $91), citing 'reduced geopolitical risk premium' and 'early signs of improving oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz' — the institutional acknowledgment that the kinetic-spike was mean-reverting. The Goldman revision aligns with Brent's $107.96 May 6 close (down from May 4 $114.44 spike). Saudi MBS Day 8 of 14 silence on UAE OPEC exit; UAE sent FM (not ruler) to May 5 Jeddah summit hosted by MBS — diplomatic distance compounds. June 7 OPEC+ ministerial is the structural test for substantive Saudi response. With UAE OUT, the post-OPEC architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait alone — fundamentally different cartel coordination math. Atlantic Council/Derentz framing for medium-term: UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity is medium-term-BEARISH-for-Brent (12-24mo). Saudi production -23% MoM (10.1 → 7.8 MBPD) structural Hormuz-export suppression continues; OPEC overall -27% MoM. The kinetic-restart May 4 → diplomatic re-pricing May 5-6 cycle did NOT alter Saudi-UAE structural divergence — May 4 strike on UAE was specifically on the OPEC defector, sequencing isn't coincidence.

current.saudi_position: carrying more burden post-UAE; MBS publicly silent; needs higher revenue post-Sadara damage
